text:
Explore (Digging In)

Providing for Individual Needs

Have Learning Aid Template: Blank 100s Grids and Learning Aid Template: Blank Grids and Tape Diagrams available for students to use as needed.

As you move around the room listening to students, take note of the groups' strategies. You will want students to share many strategies for analyzing the survey questions. This will help students to gain flexibility in thinking about how ratio reasoning is connected to percents. Tape diagrams are highlighted in this problem, but some students may prefer to use rate tables, unit rates, numeric reasoning, etc.

Students may analyze he results using equivalent ratios. If some groups finish before others, push them to use percents to verify their ratios reasoning. (problem-Solving Environment) Also, as some students try to use Keeya's strategy of a tape diagram, have them prepare a diagram to share that thinking with the class.

Not all percent problems will need to be solved using tape diagram or double number lines. Students need to become flexible scaling the percent statements, which are a type of rate. In many texts, students are asked to find the decimal equivalent for a percent. This exercise hides that a division is involved because we are finding a unit rate.
